name: hebrew-apostrophe-in-test-strings
hook: Hebrew geresh (פיצ'רים) inside single-quoted strings breaks prettier
body: Hebrew text contains a geresh (single quote variant, Unicode \u2019) which looks like an apostrophe. When Hebrew text with geresh is inside a single-quoted JavaScript string, Prettier interprets the geresh as a string terminator and fails with 'SyntaxError: comma expected'. Fix: use double-quoted strings for the outer delimiters, or escape the geresh. Example: `page.locator("a:text(\"פיצ'רים\")")` works but single-quoted `a:text('פיצ'רים')` breaks.
why: Hebrew geresh (Unicode 2019, not ASCII 27) is visually identical to an ASCII apostrophe but is a different character. Prettier correctly parses it as a string terminator when the string delimiter is ASCII single-quote, causing the rest of the string to be interpreted as new syntax.

name: inline-style-keyframes-for-landing-pages
hook: Inline keyframes via <style> tag for landing page animations
body: For landing page animations (like the float effect), adding a <style> tag with @keyframes directly in the component is acceptable and avoids modifying globals.css. The keyframes defined: @keyframes float (hero visual bobbing), @keyframes fadeInUp (onboarding bubble).
why: The landing page is a standalone experience with its own design language. Injecting the small number of keyframes it needs via <style> tag is simpler than adding to globals.css and avoids potential conflicts with existing styles.

name: landing-page-body-class-hides-header-footer
hook: body.landing-page class hides header and footer via CSS
body: The NewStartPage adds 'landing-page' class to document.body on mount (and removes on unmount). This class is defined in globals.css as `body.landing-page header, body.landing-page footer { display: none !important }`. This pattern was already used by the previous HomePage/LandingPage.
why: The new /start page has its own navigation built-in and doesn't want the site's header/footer. Using the existing body class pattern avoids duplicating nav CSS and keeps the concern at the page level.
